data.name: HVDC (High Voltage Direct Current) Transmission
data.description: Long-distance power transmission with lower losses than AC. Essential for connecting remote renewable resources to demand centers. China leads with 30+ GW HVDC lines.
data.capability: 1,100 kV, 12 GW per circuit; <3% loss per 1,000 km
data.status: Operational globally; massive expansion planned for renewable integration

data.name: Grid-Forming Inverters
data.description: Inverters that can establish grid voltage and frequency without relying on synchronous generators. Critical for 100% renewable grids.
data.capability: Replace synchronous generator functions (inertia, voltage, frequency control)
data.status: Deploying; Australia and UK leading grid-forming inverter requirements

data.name: Virtual Power Plants (VPP)
data.description: Aggregating distributed energy resources (rooftop solar, batteries, EVs, demand response) into a single dispatchable resource. Tesla, Sunrun, and utilities are deploying VPPs.
data.status: Rapidly scaling; Tesla VPP in Texas, South Australia; Sunrun + National Grid

data.name: Dynamic Line Rating
data.description: Real-time monitoring of power line capacity based on weather conditions (wind cooling, temperature). Can increase transmission capacity 15-30% without new construction.
data.status: Deploying; required by FERC in US; Ampacimon, Lindsey manufacturing leading
